DashboardOverview.overviewDesc: Universal command center for Node Operators and AI Developers.

DashboardNode.title: Operator Command Center
DashboardNode.description: Monitor your hardware delegates, network metrics, and automated tasks.
DashboardNode.stakeDesc: Deposit tokens to guarantee your honest execution of tasks.
DashboardNode.pairingDesc: Deploy a stateless Docker instance acting as your protocol orchestrator.
DashboardNode.pairingInstructions: The backend will generate a burner wallet locally, bridging it to your connected provider. No keys are stored permanently.
DashboardNode.missingKey: Missing AI Key configuration. Configure it in AI Models.
DashboardNode.confirmDepositDesc: You are about to lock {amount} SOL into the smart contract escrow to secure this node. You can request a withdrawal at any time.
DashboardNode.withdrawDesc: Withdrawing your stake will begin the unbonding process. Active tasks will be resolved, but no new tasks will be assigned. Your free stake will be available in 1 epoch.
DashboardNode.confirmStopDesc: Are you sure you want to kill the Docker container? Your node will immediately stop processing tasks, but your stake will remain locked.

DashboardApi.title: Developer Hub
DashboardApi.description: Generate SDK Keys and manage your Solarium Inference Escrow.

Explorer.subtitle: Network Infrastructure
Explorer.title: Solarium God Mode
Explorer.searchPlaceholder: Search by TX hash, Node PDA, or Task ID...
Explorer.bootstrapperDesc: Deploy automated infrastructure bots to test the autonomous network.

DashboardFaucet.title: Faucet
DashboardFaucet.description: Request testnet or localnet SOL funds directly to your connected wallet.
DashboardFaucet.networkMismatchDesc: Please switch to Devnet or Localnet to use the Faucet. Mainnet does not support airdrops.
DashboardFaucet.successMessage: Successfully airdropped {amount} SOL to your wallet.
DashboardFaucet.errorMessage: Airdrop failed. Network might be rate-limited or unavailable.
DashboardFaucet.valDesc: To run a Validator Node and participate in the LLM-as-a-Judge consensus, you need to stake exactly <amount>0.50</amount> SOL.
DashboardFaucet.workerDesc: To run an AI Worker Agent that processes GenAI inference tasks, you must lock exactly <amount>1.00</amount> SOL as collateral against hallucination.
DashboardFaucet.sandboxDesc: Creating a test task via the Explorer Sandbox costs <amount>1.50</amount> SOL, which is escrowed as the Worker/Validator rewards.

DashboardModels.title: AI Models
DashboardModels.description: Configure execution environments and language models for your protocol nodes.
DashboardModels.geminiDesc: High-performance multimodal model. Powers the initial Solarium Worker inference engine.
DashboardModels.openaiDesc: Advanced reasoning logic for validation consensus.
DashboardModels.claudeDesc: Context-heavy analysis for specialized oracle feeds.
DashboardModels.llamaDesc: Decentralized weights running locally. Absolute trust minimization.
DashboardModels.storedLocally: Keys are stored locally in your browser and injected ephemerally into your VPS worker node. We never save your API keys in our database.

HeaderLinks.appResidaoDesc: Real estate and facility tech management
HeaderLinks.docQuickstartDesc: Setup SDK and launch an AI Task

Footer.desc: Decentralized verifiable AI inference layer on Solana. Trustless execution, automated consensus.

Hero.description: Trustless LLM execution layer for Solana. Validator nodes compute deterministic outputs, commit cryptographic hashes, and secure AI logic through on-chain consensus.

ContractIntegration.subtitle: — SMART CONTRACT INTEGRATION
ContractIntegration.title: Native Anchor CPI
ContractIntegration.description: Solarium provides a native Anchor CPI interface. Request verifiable logic natively within your smart contract in just a few lines of Rust.

InferenceFlow.step1Desc: The app requests AI inference and deposits payment. Everything runs directly in your Solana smart contract, natively on L1.
InferenceFlow.step2Desc: An AI Agent (Worker) performs the task, and 3 independent verifiers confirm the response. The state becomes Optimistic Finalized.
InferenceFlow.step3Desc: The network pauses. Any global observer (Watchdog) can cross-check the AI response. If they prove an error or hallucination, they raise the alarm.
InferenceFlow.fastPathDesc: If no errors are found, the result is unconditionally accepted by the smart contract. Honest nodes claim their rewards.
InferenceFlow.slowPathDesc: On alert, a Supreme Court of 51 independent validators is summoned. If the original answer is proven fraudulent, liars permanently lose 100% of their staked funds (Slashing).

VerifiabilityGap.subtitle: Blockchains cannot think, and neural networks cannot be trusted with capital. We pioneered a secure bridge uniting AI intellect and blockchain transparency to establish next-generation smart contracts.
VerifiabilityGap.col1Desc: Smart contracts are ultra-secure but disconnected from reality. They cannot access the internet, parse vast text, or run neural networks. They operate strictly on rigid mathematical rules.
VerifiabilityGap.col2Desc: Modern AI is incredibly smart, but remains a black box. A model can hallucinate, fabricate facts, or be secretly manipulated by its creators. It cannot govern third-party capital without verifiable proofs.
VerifiabilityGap.col3Desc: Our solution: AI performs any task, and an independent validator network cross-checks its validity. If honest, the outcome is firmly committed back to Solana. Zero trust, absolute math.

ValidatorEconomics.subtitle: — VALIDATOR ECONOMICS
ValidatorEconomics.stakingDesc: Nodes must stake a minimum of SOL payload to participate in the consensus set. Prevents Sybil attacks intrinsically.
ValidatorEconomics.slashingDesc: Submitting an invalid or differing hash compared to the quorum results in instant stake slashing and eviction.
ValidatorEconomics.rewardsDesc: Valid tasks accrue base SOL fees directly to the operator's wallet address instantly post-consensus.

CtaSection.subtitle: The first builders, node operators, and validators will shape the economics of Solarium for years to come. The network rewards early participants most.

DocsHub.title: Solarium Documentation
DocsHub.subtitle: Everything you need to build on top of verifiable AI inference on Solana.
DocsHub.quickstartDesc: Install the SDK and submit your first verifiable AI task in under 5 minutes.
DocsHub.architectureDesc: Understand the Proof of Inference consensus: Workers, Validators, Commit-Reveal.
DocsHub.sdkDesc: Complete TypeScript API reference for SolariumClient: methods, types, PDA derivation.
DocsHub.nodeGuideDesc: Configure and run a Worker or Validator agent node to earn SOL rewards.

DocsQuickstart.title: Quickstart
DocsQuickstart.subtitle: Submit your first verifiable AI inference task on Solana in under 5 minutes.
DocsQuickstart.step1Desc: Add the Solarium SDK to your project.
DocsQuickstart.step2Desc: Create a SolariumClient instance using an Anchor provider.
DocsQuickstart.step3Desc: Submit an inference task to the network. The task is assigned to a Worker node.
DocsQuickstart.step4Desc: Poll the on-chain state until the Commit-Reveal cycle completes and a verdict is finalized.

DocsArchitecture.title: Protocol Architecture
DocsArchitecture.subtitle: How Solarium achieves trustless, verifiable AI inference on Solana.
DocsArchitecture.overviewDesc: Solarium is an on-chain protocol built with Anchor on Solana. It coordinates a network of Worker and Validator nodes to execute AI inference tasks and reach cryptographic consensus on the results.
DocsArchitecture.instructionsDesc: The Solarium smart contract exposes 10 instructions:
DocsArchitecture.consensusDesc: The protocol uses a two-phase commit-reveal mechanism to prevent front-running and ensure honest reporting:
DocsArchitecture.phase1: Commit Phase: Worker executes inference, computes SHA-256 hash of (verdict + confidence + salt), and submits the commitment on-chain. Validators independently verify the execution trace and commit their vote hashes.
DocsArchitecture.phase2: Reveal Phase: After the commit deadline, all parties reveal their raw values. The contract verifies each reveal matches its commitment. Mismatches trigger slashing.
DocsArchitecture.pdaDesc: All accounts are derived using Program Derived Addresses:

DocsSdk.title: SDK Reference
DocsSdk.subtitle: Complete TypeScript API for @solarium-labs/sdk.
DocsSdk.clientDesc: The main class for interacting with the Solarium protocol. Accepts an AnchorProvider in its constructor.
DocsSdk.helpersDesc: Standalone functions exported from the SDK for PDA derivation and cryptographic operations.
DocsSdk.submitsTask: Submits a new inference task to the Solarium smart contract. Funds are transferred to the escrow PDA automatically.
DocsSdk.registersNode: Registers the provider's wallet as a Worker or Validator node. Requires a minimum stake in SOL based on the role.
DocsSdk.claimsTask: (Workers Only) Claims an open task in the queue, locking it from other workers for the duration of the timeout window.

DocsNodeGuide.title: Node Operator Guide
DocsNodeGuide.subtitle: Run a Worker or Validator node to participate in verifiable AI consensus and earn SOL.
DocsNodeGuide.configDesc: Create a .env file in the agent/ directory:
DocsNodeGuide.workerDesc: Worker nodes claim open tasks, run AI inference (via Gemini or mock), and submit cryptographic commitments.
DocsNodeGuide.validatorDesc: Validator nodes independently verify worker results and vote on correctness.
DocsNodeGuide.earningsDesc: Nodes start with 5000 reputation points (max 10000). Each successful task adds 50 points. Slashing removes 200 points. Nodes below 1000 reputation cannot participate.

DocsSmartContracts.title: Smart Contract CPI
DocsSmartContracts.subtitle: Native Anchor Integration for on-chain verifiable logic.
DocsSmartContracts.cargoDesc: Add the Solarium Anchor program to your Rust smart contract. Ensure the version matches the deployed network protocol.
DocsSmartContracts.initDesc: Create an Escrow PDA natively and fund the AI inference securely, straight from your contract's logic.

DocsWorkerGuide.title: Worker Node
DocsWorkerGuide.subtitle: Get selected for tasks, run inference, earn SOL.
DocsWorkerGuide.vramDesc: Instead of bare-metal CLI, we provide a unified Desktop App where you can monitor resources and manage API keys.
DocsWorkerGuide.ramDesc: Pair your Phantom wallet from the Web Dashboard to the Windows/Mac app.
DocsWorkerGuide.fallbackDesc: If you lack powerful hardware (A100/H100), you can configure your node to offload inference to verified cloud providers like OpenAI or Gemini. The protocol only cares that you return the deterministic, honest answer.
DocsWorkerGuide.regDesc: To authorize your PC app, you must connect your Phantom Wallet via the Solarium Dashboard. Once connected, stake your SOL and pair your wallet to your running Desktop app using the generated pairing code.
DocsWorkerGuide.execDesc: Once paired, your Worker automatically scans for unassigned intelligence tasks. When it claims one, it runs the inference (Locally or via API) and submits the proof back to Solana.

DocsValidatorGuide.title: Validator Judge
DocsValidatorGuide.subtitle: Guard the truth, catch lies, claim slashed stakes.
DocsValidatorGuide.crucialDesc: Validators must accurately reproduce the worker's logic using identical or superior reasoning. You act as the Supreme Court. The temperature is forced to 0.0 for deterministic output. If you vote incorrectly alongside a minority, your stake is slashed.
DocsValidatorGuide.evalDesc: Validators must use the Solarium Web Dashboard to register. Connect your Phantom wallet, navigate to the Validator Hub, and deposit the Validator Stake (0.5 SOL). You will be issued a connection token.
DocsValidatorGuide.slashDesc: Once you input the Dashboard token into your Solarium Validator CLI, your node begins downloading claims from Workers. It uses advanced semantic-comparison algorithms to evaluate if the Worker hallucinated or told the truth.

Dashboard.connectDesc: Connect a Solana wallet to view your dashboard, node stats, and pending rewards.
Dashboard.registerDesc: Stake SOL and register your wallet as a Worker node to claim AI inference tasks and earn rewards.
Dashboard.createTaskDesc: Submit a demo inference task to the network. A Worker node will claim it and validators will verify the result.

Apps.pageSubtitle: Verifiable AI applications powered by the Solarium Protocol

residao.name: ResiDAO
residao.description: Submit your building maintenance quote to a network of AI validators. Each report is cryptographically verified on-chain. No trusted intermediaries — just consensus.

insurai.name: InsurAI
insurai.description: Submit insurance claim evidence to AI validators. Workers analyze external data sources, validators verify the reasoning. Payouts are trigger­-ready instantly after consensus.

bugbounty.name: BugBounty Net
bugbounty.description: Submit a GitHub PR or contract address. AI validators audit the code for vulnerabilities using deterministic analysis. Results are cryptographically committed on-chain.

defisentinel.name: DeFi Sentinel
defisentinel.description: AI agent reads world news in real-time. At the first signs of a crisis, the smart contract automatically saves your deposits before prices collapse.

HeroAwwwards.description: We turn blind smart contracts into autonomous AI agents. Ordinary neural networks hallucinate and make mistakes, so our network forces AI to prove its reasoning mathematically. The ultimate foundation for next-generation Web3 applications.

VerifiabilityGapAwwwards.title3: The Verifiability Gap prevents trillions of dollars from entering Web3 AI. Until today.
VerifiabilityGapAwwwards.description: Your protocol deserves true decentralization. At Solarium, we blend cryptography with LLM-as-a-Judge consensus, providing a trust-minimized, Sybil-resistant Oracle Network for any off-chain inference task.

ContractIntegrationAwwwards.feature1Desc: Solarium provides a native CPI interface. Invoke verifiable AI logic directly from your Rust smart contract in just a few lines of code.

ValidatorEconomicsAwwwards.description: Symmetrical cryptoeconomic system of checks and balances. Honest participants earn, malicious ones are penalized.
ValidatorEconomicsAwwwards.stakingDesc: Operators must lock SOL as a guarantee of honesty to participate in consensus. Effective protection against Sybil attacks.
ValidatorEconomicsAwwwards.slashingDesc: Submitting a false hash or differing from the quorum leads to an instant penalty (slashing) and eviction from the pool.
ValidatorEconomicsAwwwards.rewardsDesc: Successful validations generate base fees (BPS) in SOL, landing in the operator's balance instantly post-consensus.

DAppsShowcaseAwwwards.title: Built on Solarium
DAppsShowcaseAwwwards.residaoDesc: Protecting DAOs from corruption. AI parses market repair prices and auto-blocks inflated contractor estimates.
DAppsShowcaseAwwwards.insuraiDesc: Parametric AI Insurance. Model analyzes meteorological API data and triggers payouts to farmers instantly, without human adjusters.
DAppsShowcaseAwwwards.explorerDesc: Observe the neural consensus in real-time. Every decision, confidence score, and LLM-as-a-Judge vote is cryptographically verifiable.

SecurityArchitecture.subtitle: Solarium does not ask nodes to be honest. It makes dishonesty provably unprofitable at every level of the network.
SecurityArchitecture.m1Desc: Every Worker output is independently re-evaluated by Validators at temperature=0. The result is deterministic. No room for bias, bribery, or ambiguity. If the answer is wrong, every Validator sees the same wrong answer. Consensus is automatic.
SecurityArchitecture.m2Desc: Every node's stake is locked inside the program account. Slashing executes with strict priority before any withdrawal. Nodes cannot exit-scam the network. There is no door.
SecurityArchitecture.m3Desc: A fraudulent node loses its full stake on the first offence. Appealing costs more than the potential gain. A supreme court challenge costs more still. At each tier, the cost to continue the attack doubles. For any rational actor, the math does not work.

DAppsShowcase.description: Our protocol is open to developers worldwide. Anyone can integrate smart AI into their smart contracts today. Check out these concepts.
DAppsShowcase.app1Desc: AI cross-checks repair estimates against market prices. If a contractor tries to overcharge, the smart contract automatically blocks the excessive payment.
DAppsShowcase.app2Desc: A neural network monitors the weather via satellites. Once a drought is confirmed, affected farmers instantly receive payouts to their wallets without paperwork.
DAppsShowcase.app3Desc: An AI agent reads global crypto news in real-time. At the first signs of a market crash, the smart contract secures your investments before prices collapse.

ProtocolStack.subtitle: Solarium is an open network. Developers ship smarter products. Operators earn SOL running compute. Validators earn SOL securing results. Everyone wins when the network grows.
ProtocolStack.sdkTitle: Build AI Products People Can Trust
ProtocolStack.sdkDesc: Every application built on Solarium creates demand for compute across the network. Install the SDK, connect to the protocol, and ship AI-powered products where every output is verified on-chain. No servers. No trust assumptions. Just results your users can verify themselves.
ProtocolStack.workerDesc: Workers are the supply side of the Solarium network. Connect your machine, pick up AI tasks posted by developers, and get paid per verified result. Your staked SOL is your commitment to the network. Your uptime is your yield.
ProtocolStack.validatorDesc: Validators are the quality layer of Proof of Inference. Every AI result passes through independent review before becoming trusted on-chain. Stake SOL, audit outputs, earn protocol rewards. Catch a fraudulent result and claim the offender's entire stake.

Polygon.title: Visual Network Sandbox
Polygon.description: Drag and drop network components to build a live visual pipeline of the Solarium consensus.

StudyModePage.title: Interactive Protocol Prologue
StudyModePage.description: Understand how Solarium network operates step-by-step before running a real visual network.

step1.title: Integration & Task Configuration
step1.description: Solarium Protocol allows Web3 developers to bridge the gap between smart contracts and AI inference. In this example, a DeFi platform is integrating Solarium to verify transactions for malicious wash trading. The developer uses the Solarium SDK to construct a TaskManifest containing the user’s transaction data, the precise AI grading criteria (worker prompt), and the exact JSON schema they expect back.
step1.longText: To avoid clogging the Solana blockchain with heavy strings, the SDK uploads this Manifest to an IPFS node (like Pinata) and receives a lightweight 32-byte hash. Only this hash, along with the SOL reward (Escrow) and the required number of Validators, is actually sent to the on-chain program via client.createTask().
step1.longText2: The Solarium SDK automatically hashes the entire payload (Manifest) before sending it to the Smart Contract. This means the blockchain only stores 32 bytes, guaranteeing ultra-low transaction fees while ensuring that the AI Agents (Workers) can verifiably retrieve the exact instructions.

step2.title: 2. Real-Time DApp Interaction
step2.description: An actual end-user interacts with the DeFi platform (e.g., executing a swap). Before the transaction settles on the mainnet, the DApp intercepts the action to request an AI security audit powered by Solarium Protocol.
step2.longText1: The beauty of Solarium lies in its seamless integration into existing application workflows. When a user clicks "Confirm Swap", they don't need to know anything about AI, Validators, or Escrows. The DApp backend instantly captures the raw transaction vectors—amount, slippage tolerance, historical wallet behavior, and router path.
step2.longText2: Instead of broadcasting the transaction directly to the blockchain validator mempool (where it could execute a malicious or wash-trading exploit), the DApp puts the user action into a "pending" state. It compiles all contextual metadata into a single JSON payload. This payload serves as the raw material for the AI Workers in the next step.

step3.title: 3. Manifest Pinning & Hash Generation
step3.description: The SDK securely merges the Developer's instructions and the User's transaction payload into a unified TaskManifest, then uploads it to an IPFS node (like Pinata).
step3.longText1: Why not store this data directly on Solana? Storing 5+ KB of text on-chain requires massive rent fees in SOL. Solarium solves this using a hybrid architecture: the heavy semantic data (Prompts, User Transactions) is pinned to IPFS, ensuring it remains immutable and decentralized for essentially zero cost.
step3.longText2: When uploaded, the SDK computes a secure cryptographic hash (SHA-256). This massive JSON object is compressed into a tiny 32-byte array. This guarantees extreme security: if even a single character in the transaction is altered, the hash changes entirely, and the Smart Contract will reject it.

step4.title: 4. Trustless Escrow & State PDA
step4.description: The DApp submits the 32-byte Hash (instructions) alongside the SOL Request Bounty to the Solarium Anchor Program. The contract acts as an incorruptible judge.
step4.longText1: Why do we lock SOL in an Escrow? In decentralized networks, AI Agents need an absolute guarantee they will be paid for consuming GPU resources. Conversely, Developers need a guarantee they won't pay for hallucinated or garbage data. The Smart Contract escrows the reward (e.g., 1.5 SOL) so neither party has to trust each other. The bounty amount is dynamic and depends on task complexity.
step4.longText2: Under the hood, the Anchor program creates a unique PDA (Program Derived Address) called TaskState. This account acts as the centralized source of truth for the entire lifecycle, securely holding the SOL in a Vault until 3 independent Validator nodes reach consensus on the AI Worker's result.
step4.btnNext: Switch Context: Setup AI Worker Node

step5.title: 5. Docker Bootstrap & AI Environment
step5.description: Meanwhile, on the supply side, independent compute providers configure their Model runtimes to join the Solarium decentralized network.
step5.longText1: Solarium is completely model-agnostic. A Node Operator can choose to run local open-source models (like Llama 3 or Mistral) directly on their GPU rigs, or they can plug into commercial APIs (like Gemini, OpenAI, or Claude). For this example, the operator is bootstrapping a lightweight Docker container hooked to the Gemini API. The local Solarium Agent handles the blockchain RPC routing while the Model does the heavy lifting.
step5.longText2: Critical AI Note: Because this is a consensus network designed for Verification, the AI must act deterministically. Operators configure their models with Temperature = 0 to remove creative hallucinations. The Prompt strictly forces the output into a JSON Schema. The network only cares about cold logic and structured output. If a node's AI hallucinates, the consensus layer (Validators) will slash its stake.

step6.title: 6. Node Registration & Staking
step6.description: To become an official network participant, the Operator must lock collateral. This creates the economic security model of the protocol.
step6.longText1: Security requires skin in the game. To prevent Sybil attacks, a Worker must stake exactly 1.0 SOL (and Validators stake 0.5 SOL). If the node provides a hallucinated or incorrect result, the consensus layer will slash (destroy) 5% of this stake. If the node fails to respond in time, it loses 2%. This guarantees that only high-quality, honest compute joins the network.
step6.longText2: Security architecture: The Operator creates two wallets. An 'Operator Key' (Cold Wallet) which holds the SOL and stays completely offline. And a 'Burner Key' (Hot Wallet) which is given to the Docker container to automatically sign lightweight transaction commitments. The Burner key cannot steal the stake.

step7.title: 7. RPC Polling & Task Claiming
step7.description: With the Worker node registered and bonded, the local agent continuously scans the Solana blockchain for Open Tasks matching its Tier.
step7.longText1: The Mempool matching isn't a centralized queue; it is permissionless polling. The Docker agent pings the Solana RPC (getProgramAccounts) looking for any TaskState PDA with status = OPEN. As soon as a DApp creates a task (like the Escrow in Step 4), all registered Workers race to claim it.
step7.longText2: Claiming is a transaction. When the Worker claims the task, the Smart Contract verifies its NodeState. If valid, the contract changes the Task status to CLAIMED and locks 1.0 SOL of the Worker's stake for this specific task. This ensures the Worker is fully committed and penalizes them if they abandon it.

step8.title: 8. Off-chain Execution & Cryptographic Commit
step8.description: The Agent downloads the IPFS payload, runs the deterministic AI inference, and uploads the results. It then commits a hidden cryptographic proof to the Solana blockchain.
step8.longText1: Why Solana? A complex state machine like this (Claim → Commit → Reveal → Finalize) requires rapid multi-step interactions. On legacy blockchains, this would take 15 minutes and 0 in gas fees. On Solana, 400ms block times and sub-cent fees make real-time decentralized AI viable. Parallel execution ensures thousands of Nodes can claim and commit simultaneously without Mempool congestion.
step8.longText2: Commit-Reveal Architecture: The Worker CANNOT just send the result to the blockchain. If it did, Validators would just copy the answer to save compute costs. Instead, the Worker creates a sha256 hash: hash(Verdict + Confidence + SecretSalt). It sends ONLY this hash to Solana as a CommitState PDA. The real data is pinned to IPFS.

step9.title: 9. Reveal & AI Consensus
step9.description: Once the Worker commits, independent Validator Nodes awaken. They download the WorkerReport, run an LLM-as-a-Judge, and vote. Finally, all hashes are revealed.
step9.longText1: The Verifiable AI Pipeline: Validators do not trust the Worker. They use the committed reasoningHash to download the Worker's IPFS result. Then, they run their own deterministic prompt alongside the Worker's output to verify logical consistency. If the Worker hallucinates or tries to maliciously inject parameters, the Validators vote INVALID.
step9.longText2: Cryptographic Reveal: Once all Participants (1 Worker + N Validators) submit their shadow hashes, the Phase changes to REVEALING. Every node must now send their hidden Salt and Verdict to the smart contract. The contract re-hashes them on-chain. If anyone lies, they are slashed. If the majority agrees, the task goes to OptimisticFinalized state!

step10.title: 10. Settlement & Oracle Delivery
step10.description: The final outcome of the decentralized pipeline. Choose a scenario below to see how Solarium resolves the data back to the DApp.
step10.successText: The Protocol verified the Worker. The 1.5 SOL Escrow is distributed: 60% to Worker, 35% to Validators, 5% to Protocol. The User's Swap (from Step 2) is safely executed based on the verifiable oracle.
step10.slashText: The Worker hallucinated or tried to inject a malicious payload. Validators caught the discrepancy. The Worker's locked stake is Slashed (burned). The DApp rejects the User's swap, saving their funds from a potential drainer.
